determination.primary_reasoning: Plan criteria not met; no regulatory carve-out applies; clinical standard does not strongly support surgery.

denial_specifies_criteria.reasoning: The denial notice explicitly identifies and specifies the clinical criteria applied: 'CC-SPINE-2024 (Plan Medical Policy: Cervical Spine Surgery)' and its specific subsections '§ 2.1' and '§ 2.2'. The notice details what each criterion requires and explains how the submitted documentation failed to meet each specified criterion. This satisfies the condition requiring specification of which clinical criteria were applied.
cited_facts: case content

denial_specifies_clinical_reason.reasoning: The denial notice explicitly identifies the specific clinical facts that led to denial: no motor or sensory radicular signs documented (negative neurological exam with intact strength, reflexes, and sensation; negative Spurling's maneuver), only 4 weeks of physical therapy instead of the required 6 weeks, only intermittent ibuprofen use without trials of other pharmacological agent classes, and no interventional procedures attempted. These are not merely restatements of criteria but specific factual findings from the patient's clinical record that explain why each criterion was not met.

denial_provides_imr_rights.reasoning: The denial notice contains a dedicated section titled 'RIGHT TO INDEPENDENT MEDICAL REVIEW' that explicitly informs the member of their right to request an IMR under California Health & Safety Code § 1374.30 and § 1374.31, provides contact information for the California Department of Managed Health Care (phone and website), and specifies the 6-month filing deadline. This satisfies the requirement that the denial notice include notice of the member's IMR rights.

cervical_radiculopathy_diagnosed.reasoning: The condition requires confirmed diagnosis of cervical radiculopathy with nerve root compression and radicular symptoms (pain, numbness, weakness in dermatomal distribution). The clinical record explicitly states 'No radiculopathy documented,' the neurological examination shows no dermatomal sensory loss, normal motor strength throughout, negative Spurling's test, and the MRI shows 'no nerve root impingement' and 'no significant neural compromise.' The absence of radicular symptoms in a dermatomal distribution and explicit negative findings for radiculopathy and nerve root compression mean the condition is not satisfied.
cited_facts: [extract] case content (confidence 0.80): Chronic axial cervical pain with bilateral shoulder girdle pain. Mild multilevel cervical spondylosis (C4-C5, C5-C6, C6-C7 disc desiccation). No radiculopathy documented. No myelopathy documented. No disc herniation with neurological deficit documented.

[extract] case content (confidence 0.80): Examination dated 2026-03-18 by Dr. Elena Rodriguez: Mental status intact. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Motor: strength 5/5 throughout bilateral upper extremities including deltoid, biceps, triceps, wrist extension, finger flexion, intrinsic hand muscles. Sensation: intact to light touch, pinprick, and proprioception throughout C4-T1 distributions bilaterally. No dermatomal sensory loss documented. Reflexes: biceps 2+ bilaterally, triceps 2+ bilaterally, brachioradialis 2+ bilaterally. No Hoffmann sign. No clonus. Spurling's test negative bilaterally. Shoulder abduction relief sign negative. Range of motion: cervical flexion 50°, extension 40°, lateral rotation 70° bilaterally, lateral flexion 35° bilaterally with end-range discomfort. Tenderness to palpation over bilateral paraspinal muscles C5-T1 level and bilateral trapezii. Adson's maneuver negative.

[extract] case content (confidence 0.80): MRI cervical spine, 2026-01-14: Mild multilevel cervical spondylosis. C4-C5: mild disc desiccation, small posterior bulge, no significant central or foraminal stenosis. C5-C6: moderate disc desiccation, small broad-based posterior disc bulge measuring 1.5mm, no central stenosis, mild bilateral neural foraminal narrowing without nerve root impingement. C6-C7: mild disc desiccation, no significant bulge. No evidence of disc herniation, no central canal stenosis, no severe foraminal stenosis, no nerve root impingement, no cord compression, no myelomalacia signal. Impression: Mild multilevel cervical degenerative changes without significant neural compromise.

cervical_myelopathy_diagnosed.reasoning: The condition requires a confirmed diagnosis of cervical myelopathy with spinal cord compression and specific myelopathic symptoms. The medical records explicitly document 'No myelopathy documented,' MRI shows 'no cord compression,' clinical examination shows normal reflexes (2+), 'No Hoffmann sign,' and normal motor strength (5/5 bilaterally). None of the required myelopathic symptoms (gait disturbance, bilateral upper extremity weakness, hyperreflexia, positive Hoffman sign, bowel/bladder dysfunction) are present.

pt_contraindicated_or_futile.reasoning: The condition requires written documentation from the treating physician that physical therapy is contraindicated or unlikely to benefit the member. Dr. Rodriguez's documentation does not include any such attestation; rather, it describes the patient as having tried conservative measures (including PT) without lasting relief, and the PT records show the patient improved during treatment (pain decreased from 7/10 to 4/10) before self-discontinuing early. The absence of documented contraindication or futility assessment means the condition is not satisfied.

functional_plateau_documented.reasoning: The condition requires both completion of a PT course AND documentation of functional plateau without adequate symptom resolution. The patient discontinued therapy before completing the planned course, and the treating PT explicitly documented 'continued expected progress' and 'making expected gains' at discontinuation, which directly contradicts the functional plateau definition requiring documented failure to achieve meaningful improvement.
cited_facts: [extract] case content (confidence 0.80): Discharge summary, Westside Rehabilitation, 2025-09-02: Patient discontinued before completing planned 6-week course. Final session pain rating 4/10 (initial 7/10). Cervical range of motion improved by approximately 15% bilaterally. Patient declined further sessions. Discharge status: improvement noted, plan of care not completed.

[extract] case content (confidence 0.80): Progress notes consistent with discharge summary. No documentation of plateau, contraindication, or futility. Treating PT documented continued expected progress at time of discontinuation. structural_neurological_compromise.reasoning: The condition requires objective evidence of structural neurological compromise that PT cannot address, such as cord compression with neurological deficit, significant herniation causing radiculopathy, or myelopathy. The MRI shows no disc herniation, no central canal stenosis, no nerve root impingement, no cord compression, and no myelomalacia. The clinical examination reveals normal strength, sensation, reflexes, and negative provocative tests with no radiculopathy or myelopathy documented. The case presents only mild degenerative changes without significant neural compromise.

clinical_standard_supports_surgery.reasoning: Per tier_3_criteria definition, TIER 3 applies to 'axial neck pain without radiculopathy or myelopathy' or 'imaging findings without correlating symptoms.' The patient presents with chronic axial cervical pain and shoulder pain, but examination shows no radiculopathy (negative Spurling's, 5/5 strength, intact sensation throughout all dermatomes, no myelopathic signs), and MRI shows no nerve root impingement or cord compression. This is axial pain without radiculopathy or myelopathy, which per the condition falls squarely into TIER 3 (surgery not clinically indicated).
